item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's comments like these that keep me writing sometimes ^_^ Thank you <3

I suspect the unicorn is really just a donkey with an ice cream cone on its head but once I catch it again I'll let you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is delightful, though how you manage to come up with original anomalies without going abstract and bizarre is beyond me.

+1, pending the release of the authorial wish-granting unicorn you've got chained up in your basement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent read, I very much enjoyed it. Thank you for sharing it with us.

+ Review
- Review

I think that the overall piece paints a lovely picture of life, love, and sacrifice. The emotion between the two brothers is clearly felt, even as a description of what I imagine are period-appropriate crude cave paintings. I assume that the two brothers maintain their vigil over each other as the smilodons? I enjoyed the fact that their "bodies" also included more recent weaponry, as an example of their struggle against various other peoples.

A question, however. If the guardians only react when someone touches the paintings, how were the paintings dated? Same goes for them, since I understand the process of radiocarbon dating involves at least minor damage due to sample collection.

I don't necessarily think that the piece should be revised to include this information, but it is something that stuck out at me.

As I said, this was quite lovely. An easy +1 from me.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to MalyceGraves or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It's simple, elegant, and the story comes through cleanly. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woot woot.

Dedicated to my brother, I guess. (He's neither dead nor a cat person, go figure).

Thanks to westrin, bleep, and henz for helping.

This is, essentially, my spiritual rewrite reimagining of the original SCP-577.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cueva_R%C3%ADo_Secreto_(1).jpg CC 4.0
